applied_optimizations.name: 2D blocked Triton kernel with AMD tuning
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented 2D blocked matrix multiplication with BLOCK_M=64, BLOCK_N=64, BLOCK_K=32, num_warps=4, num_stages=1. Each program handles a 64x64 tile, iterating over L dimension in 8 blocks of 32.

attempted_but_failed.name: FP16 compute
attempted_but_failed.reason: Precision issues - max diff 0.009, correctness failed

attempted_but_failed.name: Autotune with fixed large grid
attempted_but_failed.reason: Slower at 7.62ms, too many idle programs

top_bottlenecks: Memory bandwidth for loading A and B tensors
8 iterations over L dimension with BLOCK_K=32

notes: Best performance: 3.81ms vs 3.02ms PyTorch reference (0.79x speedup, SCORE: 50)
AMD-specific tuning: num_warps=4, num_stages=1 is optimal
PyTorch einsum uses highly optimized rocBLAS/hipBLASLt - extremely difficult to beat with naive Triton
Block size 64x64x32 provides best balance of parallelism and memory efficiency
FP16 compute attempted but failed correctness due to precision loss
Stage 1 complete: baseline established at SCORE 50 (correct but not faster)
